mainEntity.name: What is the main advantage of a standing seam metal roof?

acceptedAnswer.text: The hidden‑fastener system eliminates exposed screws, delivering up to 220 mph wind uplift and a 50‑year lifespan.

mainEntity.name: Can I install a standing seam metal roof myself?

acceptedAnswer.text: Installation needs specialized tools for hand‑crimping and precise cuts; professional installers ensure compliance with high‑velocity hurricane zone code.

mainEntity.name: How does panel material affect cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: 24‑gauge steel is standard; aluminum costs about double, and copper can be five times more per square foot.

mainEntity.name: What is oil canning and how is it prevented?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oil canning is a ripple effect on flat metal; striated panels and proper seam spacing minimize it.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a special underlayment?

acceptedAnswer.text: High‑velocity hurricane zones require double XFR (80 mil) underlayment, adding roughly $150‑$200 per square foot.

mainEntity.name: How many clips are used per panel?

acceptedAnswer.text: Clips are spaced every 4‑6 in; tighter spacing raises wind uplift ratings.
